response.justification: A versatile, experienced polyglot engineer with directly relevant geospatial/GIS background (GISCollective, Green Map) and strong low-latency distributed-systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/sec with async I/O, fibers, and caching at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic). However, the role's primary hard requirement is modern C++ or Rust, and the candidate's C/C++ exposure is limited and dated (~6 months at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a thesis project), with no Rust. Recent work is overwhelmingly TypeScript/Node.js. The systems instincts and stated willingness to work beyond current expertise fit the JD's emphasis on cross-stack growth, making this a borderline case worth a screening conversation to gauge C++ ramp-up.
strengths: Strong geospatial/GIS domain experience (GISCollective, Green Map) directly relevant to navigation data
Proven low-latency, high-throughput distributed-systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching at scale)
Highly adaptable polyglot with Linux/systems background and demonstrated appetite to learn new stacks
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and ~10 years old; no Rust experience
No direct navigation/routing or production map-pipeline experience
Recent career focus (TypeScript/Node.js) diverges from the role's core systems-language requirement
